pathophysiology_summary: Sudden plaque rupture with thrombotic occlusion (partial or complete) of a coronary artery produces myocardial ischaemia. Crushing central chest pain radiating to the left arm and jaw reflects shared C7-T4 cardiac and somatic dermatomal referral. Profuse diaphoresis and a sense of impending doom (angor animi) reflect intense sympathetic/parasympathetic activation accompanying significant ischaemia. Resting dyspnoea suggests possible early left ventricular dysfunction. Without urgent reperfusion (primary PCI for STEMI, or urgent risk-stratified management for NSTEMI), ongoing occlusion causes progressive irreversible myocardial necrosis and risk of fatal ventricular arrhythmia. Time to reperfusion is the dominant determinant of myocardial salvage and survival — this is the definitive time-critical emergency and the rural/outer-regional location makes early ambulance dispatch even more critical.

differential.evidence_basis: Crushing central chest pain with jaw radiation at rest is a classic presentation.
Must always be screened for in severe chest pain (tearing quality, interscapular radiation, pulse/BP asymmetry) even though the described crushing/radiating pattern favours ACS.
Dyspnoea and chest discomfort warrant PE screening, though crushing central pain with jaw radiation and diaphoresis favour a cardiac cause.
Diaphoresis, radiation to arm/jaw, and sense of impending doom are hallmarks of cardiac ischaemia rather than reflux, and should not be accepted as an alternative given this severity.

differential_progression.scoring_note: This presentation is textbook ACS from the opening complaint and should already trigger emergency-pathway thinking; aortic dissection and PE remain important screens but should not delay 000 dispatch.

differential.evidence_basis: Jaw/arm radiation (DI-001), diaphoresis with sense of impending doom (DI-002), and resting dyspnoea (DI-003) together confirm a high-probability ACS constellation.
No tearing/ripping quality or interscapular radiation described; crushing anterior pattern is more consistent with ACS, though cannot be fully excluded without imaging.
Crushing (not pleuritic) pain pattern and absence of risk factors elicited make PE less likely, though not formally excluded via telehealth.

differential_progression.scoring_note: By this stage ACS should be the sole leading diagnosis with immediate emergency escalation, not further exploratory questioning.

differential.evidence_basis: Working diagnosis for telehealth context; confirmation requires 12-lead ECG and serial troponin, both unavailable remotely. Emergency escalation is the only appropriate action.

differential_progression.scoring_note: A management plan without immediate 000 dispatch and pre-hospital aspirin advice at this stage is a critical failure.

red_flags.description: Crushing central chest pain radiating to left arm and jaw
red_flags.clinical_significance: Classic cardiac referral pattern via shared dermatomes; among the most specific historical features for ACS.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Patient reassured and told to rest; ongoing occlusion causes myocardial necrosis or fatal arrhythmia.

red_flags.description: Profuse diaphoresis with sense of impending doom (angor animi)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Marker of intense autonomic activation accompanying significant myocardial ischaemia.

red_flags.description: Resting dyspnoea accompanying the chest pain
red_flags.clinical_significance: May indicate early left ventricular dysfunction/pulmonary congestion; raises overall acuity.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Cardiac cause of dyspnoea not considered.

red_flags.description: ST-segment elevation/depression on 12-lead ECG and elevated high-sensitivity troponin (clinician/equipment-only findings)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Definitive diagnostic criteria for STEMI/NSTEMI; require ECG and pathology equipment unavailable via telehealth chat.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Not directly available to the AI Doctor — escalation must rely on the classic historical tetrad above.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Patient's rural location and reluctance to 'bother anyone' may tempt an AI Doctor to suggest waiting or self-transport rather than immediate 000 dispatch — this must be actively countered given time-critical myocardial salvage.
Failing to advise chewing aspirin (300mg) while awaiting the ambulance, if available and no contraindication, delays antiplatelet therapy that reduces infarct extension.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Emergency ambulance (000), Priority 1 dispatch given rural/outer-regional location and time-critical myocardial salvage window
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Any delay — advising the patient to wait, rest, or arrange non-emergency transport — risks fatal ventricular arrhythmia or irreversible myocardial necrosis.
